worth_it_explanation: Incremental reach is the measurement question that justifies a cross-screen media mix: which households did this channel reach that the others did not? Without it, a planner cannot tell whether digital or connected TV (CTV) spend is extending the audience beyond what linear TV already covered, or whether it is re-reaching the same households at greater cost. These checks confirm that incremental reach is being measured at all, that the figures are being used to inform budget allocation across channels, and that the linear TV exposure data feeding the calculation is complete enough to produce a reliable result.
intro: Samba TV uses Automatic Content Recognition (ACR) data from opt-in smart TVs to measure cross-screen advertising reach. One of its core use cases is incremental reach: the unique households exposed to a digital or CTV campaign leg that were not already reached by the linear TV buy, and vice versa. This figure tells a planner how much net-new audience each channel adds on top of the others. When incremental reach is not measured, not acted on, or built on incomplete linear exposure data, the cross-screen strategy cannot be evaluated or optimized on its actual audience extension. These checks examine whether the campaign team is measuring incremental reach, using it to inform channel allocation, and supplying the linear TV exposure data that the calculation depends on.

audit_checks.title: Incremental reach is not measured, so the campaign cannot show how much net-new audience each channel adds beyond the linear TV buy
audit_checks.impact_rationale: Without an incremental reach measurement, a planner has no way to determine whether digital or CTV spend is reaching households that linear TV did not already cover, or whether the same households are being reached multiple times across channels at combined cost. The cross-screen budget cannot be evaluated against its stated purpose of audience extension.

educate.base:
Samba TV's Automatic Content Recognition (ACR) data, collected from opted-in smart TV households, identifies which households were exposed to linear TV content and advertising within a given campaign window. When combined with CTV and digital exposure data, this creates the foundation for a cross-screen, deduplicated reach measurement. Incremental reach is the subset of that measurement: the households exposed to one channel leg that had no exposure to the other leg.

A planner running linear TV, CTV, and digital video as part of a single campaign is typically making one of two assumptions: that CTV and digital are reaching new households not already covered by linear, or that they are deepening frequency among households that have already seen the linear ad. Incremental reach measurement from Samba TV distinguishes between those two outcomes. Without it, both assumptions are guesses, and budget allocation between channels cannot be justified by audience extension data.

Incremental reach requires the campaign to be set up as a cross-screen measurement study in Samba TV with all relevant channels in scope. A study that measures only linear TV or only CTV, without comparing the two, produces reach figures for each channel in isolation but cannot calculate how much audience each adds beyond the other. The cross-screen deduplication is the mechanism that produces the incremental figure.

A few signs this check applies to the campaign:
  • The Samba TV report shows reach and frequency figures for each channel separately but does not include a cross-screen reach total or an incremental reach breakdown
  • The measurement study was set up as a single-channel study rather than a cross-screen study, so no comparison between linear and digital or CTV exposure is possible
  • The campaign brief includes audience extension as a goal but the Samba TV report contains no data supporting or refuting whether extension was achieved
You may want to confirm whether the Samba TV study was configured as a cross-screen measurement study before the campaign launched, and whether the report includes an incremental reach section alongside the per-channel reach figures.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a retail campaign running linear TV to drive broad awareness and CTV or digital video to reach cord-cutters or lighter TV viewers, incremental reach is the metric that shows whether the CTV and digital spend reached households the linear buy did not. You may want to confirm the Samba TV study is set up to produce that comparison before the next campaign flight.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation campaign supplementing a linear TV buy with digital video, incremental reach tells you how much of the digital spend extended the audience beyond those already exposed on linear. You may want to confirm the Samba TV study compares linear and digital exposure rather than measuring each channel in isolation.

learn_more.source_title: MRC Cross-Media Audience Measurement Standards, Phase I Video

educate.learn_more_note: Authoritative guidance on Samba TV incremental reach measurement configuration is in the gated Samba TV measurement documentation. Contact your Samba TV account team for access to the cross-screen study setup guide.

investigate.intro: Review the Samba TV report and study configuration to determine whether cross-screen incremental reach is included in the output.

samba_tv.label: Samba TV
steps: Log in to your Samba TV reporting portal and open the report for the campaign you are auditing.

Review the report's reach section. Confirm whether it includes a cross-screen or deduplicated total reach figure and an incremental reach breakdown showing how much audience each channel added beyond the others.

If the report shows only per-channel reach figures with no cross-screen comparison, navigate to the study's configuration and confirm whether it was set up as a cross-screen study or a single-channel study.

Confirm that the channels included in the study match all channels that ran in the campaign. A study with fewer channels than the media plan cannot produce incremental reach for the excluded channels.
Note whether the study configuration was finalized before the campaign launched. A study configured after delivery has started may have missed exposure data from the early part of the flight.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records whether the Samba TV report includes a cross-screen incremental reach figure that compares linear TV exposure to digital or CTV exposure. If the report includes an incremental reach section with a breakdown by channel, the check passes. If the report shows only per-channel reach in isolation with no cross-screen comparison, or if the study was set up as a single-channel measurement, the check is a fail, recorded with the study type and the channels included in the measurement scope.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the study type (cross-screen or single-channel) and whether the report includes a cross-screen incremental reach figure above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that the Samba TV measurement for {{site_name}}'s campaign does not include cross-screen incremental reach, so there is no data showing how much net-new audience each channel added beyond the linear TV buy. The campaign cannot be evaluated against an audience extension goal without that figure, and budget allocation across linear, CTV, and digital cannot be justified by reach data. {{voice_recommend}} configuring the Samba TV study as a cross-screen measurement that includes all campaign channels before the next flight so the report produces an incremental reach breakdown alongside the per-channel figures.

audit_checks.title: Incremental reach is measured but not used to rebalance spend across channels, so budget continues flowing to channels that add little net-new audience
audit_checks.impact_rationale: An incremental reach report that is not connected to budget decisions leaves the planning process unchanged. Spend may continue at the same proportion across channels even when the measurement shows that one channel is adding minimal net-new audience and another is producing a larger share of unique household coverage.

educate.base:
Incremental reach from Samba TV measures the unique households that one channel reached beyond what the other channels already covered. It produces a figure for each channel leg: the share of that channel's reach that was truly additive to the media mix versus the share that duplicated reach from another channel. That breakdown is useful for planning only when it influences how dollars are allocated across channels in the next campaign flight.

A channel with high reach overlap against linear TV, meaning most of the households it reached had already been exposed on linear, contributes to frequency rather than audience extension. If the campaign's goal is to extend reach beyond the linear audience, that channel is not delivering on the goal efficiently. Reallocating dollars toward a channel with higher incremental reach against linear would produce more net-new audience coverage for the same total spend.

This analysis requires connecting the Samba TV incremental reach output to the media plan for the next flight. It does not happen automatically. Someone on the planning or analytics team needs to take the incremental reach figures, calculate the cost per incremental household for each channel, and use that comparison to inform the channel weight in the next plan.

A few signs the incremental reach data is not being used for allocation decisions:
  • The campaign's channel mix and budget proportions are unchanged from the prior flight despite incremental reach data being available from Samba TV for the previous campaign
  • Post-campaign reviews reference the incremental reach figure as a performance metric but do not include a recommendation for how the channel mix should change
  • The planning team is not aware that a cost-per-incremental-household comparison is possible from the Samba TV report
You may want to confirm whether the post-campaign review includes a channel allocation recommendation based on incremental reach efficiency, and whether that recommendation carries into the next media plan.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a retailer running linear TV, CTV, and digital video with a goal of reaching cord-cutters not covered by linear, you may want to confirm the post-campaign analysis calculates a cost per incremental household for each channel and that the result shapes the budget split for the following flight.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser using TV to build awareness, you may want to confirm that the Samba TV incremental reach data from the previous campaign was reviewed before the current plan was finalized, and that any channel with high overlap against linear was either reduced in weight or assigned to a different audience extension objective.

educate.learn_more_note: Authoritative guidance on applying Samba TV incremental reach data to media planning is in the gated Samba TV measurement documentation. Contact your Samba TV account team for access to the measurement reporting guide.

investigate.intro: Review the post-campaign analysis and the current media plan to determine whether incremental reach data from Samba TV has been used to inform channel budget allocation.

samba_tv.label: Samba TV
steps: Log in to your Samba TV reporting portal and open the report for the most recent completed campaign flight.

Locate the incremental reach section. Note the incremental reach figure for each channel and, if available, the percentage of each channel's reach that was additive versus duplicated against other channels.
Pull the media plan or insertion order for the subsequent campaign flight. Compare the channel budget weights to those of the prior flight.

Ask the planning or analytics team whether the Samba TV incremental reach figures from the prior campaign were reviewed before the current plan was finalized. Ask whether any channel weight was adjusted based on incremental reach efficiency.

If cost-per-incremental-household figures are not already in the post-campaign report, calculate them from the incremental reach count and the channel spend for the prior flight, then compare across channels.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records whether incremental reach data from Samba TV was reviewed as part of the planning process for the current or most recent campaign flight, and whether any budget reallocation based on incremental reach efficiency was documented. If the post-campaign review includes a cost-per-incremental-household comparison by channel and the current plan reflects an adjustment based on that analysis, the check passes. If incremental reach data exists from a prior flight but was not used in planning, the check is a fail, recorded with the channels' incremental reach figures and the channel proportions that remained unchanged.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record whether incremental reach data from the prior flight was reviewed in planning and whether any channel budget weight changed as a result above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that {{site_name}}'s campaign team is measuring incremental reach through Samba TV but the figures are not being used to inform budget allocation across channels. The cross-screen measurement shows how much net-new audience each channel added beyond the linear TV buy, but the channel weights in the media plan have not changed in response to that data. {{voice_recommend}} reviewing the cost per incremental household for each channel from the Samba TV report and using that comparison to adjust the channel split in the next flight, shifting spend toward the channels delivering the highest proportion of unique household coverage.

audit_checks.title: The linear TV exposure data used to calculate incremental reach is missing or incomplete, making the incremental reach figure unreliable
audit_checks.impact_rationale: Incremental reach is calculated by comparing which households were exposed on linear TV against which households were exposed on CTV or digital. If the linear TV exposure data is incomplete, the comparison baseline is wrong and the incremental reach figure overstates or understates how much net-new audience each channel added. Planning decisions made from an unreliable incremental reach figure may move budget in the wrong direction.

educate.base:
Samba TV measures linear TV exposure through Automatic Content Recognition (ACR) data captured by its smart TV panel. ACR identifies which content and ads an opted-in household was exposed to during a given time window by matching audio or video fingerprints from the TV's output against a reference library. The quality and completeness of the linear TV exposure data depends on two things: the size and demographic representativeness of the Samba TV panel in the relevant market, and whether the linear TV airings were fully logged in the reference library used for fingerprint matching.

When linear TV exposure data is incomplete, the baseline for incremental reach is incorrect. If Samba TV's panel undercounts linear exposure in a given market or daypart, some households that were actually reached by linear will appear as if they were not, and those households will be counted as incremental reach for the digital or CTV leg. The incremental reach figure will be higher than it should be, and the conclusion that digital or CTV added a large unique audience may be an artifact of missing linear data rather than a real audience extension effect.

This issue surfaces most clearly when the linear TV exposure count in the Samba TV report appears low relative to the GRP (gross rating points) or reach figure that the TV network or agency reported for the same buy. A large gap between those two sources may indicate that the Samba TV panel did not capture the full linear exposure, or that the reference library did not match all of the airings.

A few signs the linear exposure data may be incomplete:
  • The Samba TV linear TV reach figure is substantially lower than the reach figure reported by the TV network, Nielsen, or the agency for the same flight and time period
  • The incremental reach percentage for digital or CTV is unusually high relative to what prior campaigns or industry benchmarks would suggest
  • The Samba TV report notes a low panel match rate or a gap in fingerprint matching for specific dayparts or networks where the campaign ran
You may want to compare the Samba TV linear reach figure against an independent source, such as the network-reported reach or a Nielsen figure for the same buy, before relying on the incremental reach calculation.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a retail campaign with heavy linear TV weight in a specific regional market, you may want to confirm the Samba TV panel has adequate representation in that market before treating the incremental reach figure as a reliable measure of how much the digital buy extended the local audience.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation campaign using linear TV to reach a specific demographic, you may want to compare the Samba TV linear reach figure for that demographic against the network's reported delivery before concluding that digital added a specific quantity of incremental reach within that target group.

learn_more.source_title: MRC Cross-Media Audience Measurement Standards, Phase I Video

educate.learn_more_note: Authoritative guidance on Samba TV panel coverage, ACR matching methodology, and linear TV data completeness is in the gated Samba TV measurement documentation. Contact your Samba TV account team for market-level panel coverage information.

investigate.intro: Compare the Samba TV linear TV reach figure against an independent benchmark to assess whether the linear exposure baseline is complete enough to support a reliable incremental reach calculation.

samba_tv.label: Samba TV
steps: Log in to your Samba TV reporting portal and open the report for the campaign you are auditing.
Note the linear TV reach figure and the incremental reach breakdown showing what share of CTV or digital reach was additive beyond linear.
Pull the linear TV delivery report from the agency, network, or Nielsen for the same campaign and time period. Note the reach figure reported for the same target audience or geography.

Compare the two linear reach figures. If the Samba TV linear reach figure is materially lower than the network or Nielsen figure, note the percentage gap and flag it as a potential data completeness issue.
If the Samba TV report includes a panel coverage or match rate indicator for the relevant markets or dayparts, note whether any gaps are flagged there.

Ask the Samba TV account team whether the ACR reference library included all networks and dayparts where the linear buy ran, particularly for local or regional buys where network-level fingerprint coverage may differ from national campaigns.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records the Samba TV linear TV reach figure and an independent benchmark reach figure from the network, agency, or Nielsen for the same flight. If the two figures are within a reasonable range and no panel coverage or match rate gaps are flagged in the Samba TV report, the check passes. If the Samba TV linear reach figure is substantially lower than the benchmark, or if the report notes a gap in panel coverage or ACR matching for relevant markets or dayparts, the check is a fail, recorded with both reach figures and the percentage gap between them.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the Samba TV linear TV reach figure and an independent benchmark figure for the same flight above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} a gap between the linear TV reach figure in the Samba TV report for {{site_name}}'s campaign and the reach figure reported by the network or agency for the same buy. The incremental reach calculation compares digital and CTV exposure against the linear baseline, so if the linear baseline undercounts actual exposure, the incremental figure overstates how much net-new audience the other channels added. Planning decisions made from that figure may shift budget in the wrong direction. {{voice_recommend}} comparing the Samba TV linear reach figure against an independent benchmark before relying on incremental reach for channel allocation, and discussing panel coverage and ACR match rate for the relevant markets and dayparts with the Samba TV account team.
